text: fat is 1.2g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, fibre and practicality are supposed to carry the case.
carbs is 62.6g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
protein is 21.4g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
vitamin e at 1% DV, vitamin c at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 41% DV, iron at 29% DV, potassium at 30% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

pros first. filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

the drawbacks next. can be rough on digestion for some people because practical tolerance still matters. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. protein quality still has a ceiling because good, but not meat or dairy level.
A tier. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
